papers.title: Strike on Sudan hospital kills at least 64 and wounds 89 more, WHO reports
papers.body_text: A strike on a healthcare facility in Sudan has killed 64 people and wounded 89 more, the World Health Organization reported on Saturday. The UN’s humanitarian office in Sudan had earlier said it was “appalled by the attack on a hospital in East Darfur yesterday, reportedly killing dozens, including children, and injuring more”. Sudanese rights group the Emergency Lawyers, who document atrocities in the war between Sudan’s army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, reported it was an army drone strike that hit the El-Daein teaching hospital. The RSF dominates the vast western Darfur region, while the army is in control of Sudan’s east, centre and north. The WHO’s surveillance system for attacks marked Friday’s incident as “confirmed” but did not give an exact location. The attack involved “violence with heavy weapons” and affected a secondary health care facility, medical personnel, patients, supplies and storage, the record showed. Though the WHO counts and verifies attacks on health care, it does not attribute blame, as it is not an investigative agency. El-Daein, the RSF-controlled state capital of East Darfur, has been regularly attacked by the army, which is trying to push the paramilitary back towards its Darfur strongholds and away from Sudan’s central corridor. Its most recent strike on the city’s market earlier this month set fire to oil barrels that burned for hours. Near-daily drone strikes are now a hallmark of Sudan’s brutal war, killing dozens at a time, mostly in the southern Kordofan region. The UN human rights chief, Volker Türk, this month said he was “appalled” after more than 200 civilians were reported killed by drone attacks within an eight-day period. “Parties to the conflict in Sudan continue to use increasingly powerful drones to deploy explosive weapons with wide-area impacts in populated areas,” he said. To the repeated condemnation of the UN, hospitals have been a regular target throughout the war. By December, more than 1,800 people had been killed in attacks on health facilities since the start of the war, including 173 health workers, according to the UN. This year, a total of 12 attacks on health care in Sudan have been recorded, causing 178 deaths and 237 injuries. Across the country, the war has killed tens of thousands and driven more than 11 million people from their homes. It has fuelled what the UN describes as the world’s largest displacement and hunger crises, with more than 33 million people in need of humanitarian aid.

questions.stem: The UN’s humanitarian office in Sudan expressed deep shock, using the word "______" to describe their reaction to the attack on the hospital.

options.A: appalled
options.B: apathetic
options.C: ambiguous
options.D: applicable

questions.explanation: 根据原文“appalled by the attack on a hospital”，appalled意为“极其震惊的/愤慨的”，符合语境中联合国机构对袭击医院行为的谴责态度。

questions.stem: The World Health Organization does not point fingers or assign ______ for the attacks because it is not an investigative body.

options.A: merit
options.B: blame
options.C: tribute
options.D: caution

questions.explanation: 原文提到“it does not attribute blame”，意为“不归咎责任”，blame符合语义逻辑，即WHO作为非调查机构，不对袭击方的责任进行定性。

questions.stem: Near-daily drone strikes have become a ______ of Sudan’s brutal war, appearing frequently in the conflict.

options.A: hallmark
options.B: byproduct
options.C: milestone
options.D: counterpart

questions.explanation: hallmark意为“标志/显著特征”，文中指无人机袭击已成为苏丹残酷战争的一个典型特征，符合句意。

questions.stem: Sudanese warring factions are using increasingly powerful drones to deploy weapons in ______ areas, leading to mass civilian casualties.

options.A: remote
options.B: populated
options.C: vacant
options.D: secluded

questions.explanation: 文中提到在“populated areas”（人口稠密地区）使用爆炸性武器，这解释了为何造成大量平民伤亡，populated在此处最为准确。

questions.stem: The ongoing war in Sudan has ______ what the UN describes as the world’s largest displacement and hunger crises.

options.A: fuelled
options.B: alleviated
options.C: discarded
options.D: contained

questions.explanation: fuelled意为“加剧/刺激”，指战争导致了严重的人道主义危机。alleviated（缓解）与文意相反，discarded（抛弃）和contained（包含/控制）逻辑不通。

papers.title: Satellite lofted for first Arab country
papers.body_text: Algeria's first communications satellite is launched on Monday. JU ZHENHUA/XINHUA A Chinese-made communications satellite became the first "export" of such a spacecraft to an Arab country when it was launched by China on Monday for Algeria. A Long March 3B carrier rocket blasted off at the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the southwestern province of Sichuan at 12:40 am. It soared for about 26 minutes before placing the Alcomsat-1, the Algeria's first communications satellite, into geostationary transfer orbit, according to a news release from China Great Wall Industry Corp, the project contractor. President Xi Jinping and his Algerian counterpart Abdelaziz Bouteflika exchanged congratulatory messages on Monday morning on the successful mission. In his message, Xi said that the satellite project is an important manifestation of the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership. It has become a good example of space cooperation between China and Arab countries and will play a significant role in promoting Algeria's economic and social development, he said. Xi said that Beijing is willing to work with Algeria to strengthen exchanges and cooperation in various fields, deepening the bilateral partnership so as to benefit the two countries and peoples. Bouteflika said that Alcomsat-1 is a remarkable achievement in space cooperation between the two countries and reflects the deep traditional friendship between the two sides. Algeria is willing to work with China to strengthen cooperation, he said. The satellite contract was signed in December 2013 between the Algerian Space Agency and China Great Wall Industry Corp, the country's largest space contractor in the international market. Alcomsat-1 was designed and manufactured by the China Academy of Space Technology, part of China Aerospace Science and Technology Corp, based on the DFH-4 satellite platform. With a liftoff weight of 5.2 metric tons and a designed life of 15 years, the satellite will be used for a wide variety of public services such as television broadcasting, emergency communications, remote education and satellite-based signal augmentation, according to Great Wall Industry. Fu Zhiheng, vice-president of the company, said Alcomsat-1 is as good as Western communications satellites in terms of technology and capacity. He said there are other contracts between his firm and Arab countries, without elaborating. zhaolei@chinadaily.com.cn

questions.stem: What is the primary significance of the Alcomsat-1 launch mentioned in the text?

options.A: It marks the first time China has exported a spacecraft to an Arab country.
options.B: It is the first satellite ever launched by the Long March 3B carrier rocket.
options.C: It symbolizes the official start of a space competition between China and Western nations.
options.D: It serves as the first instance of Algeria collaborating with a space-capable country.

questions.explanation: 文章第一段明确指出：A Chinese-made communications satellite became the first 'export' of such a spacecraft to an Arab country。故A项正确。

questions.stem: How did President Xi Jinping characterize the Alcomsat-1 project?

options.A: As a purely financial venture between two commercial entities.
options.B: As a critical step in China's military expansion in North Africa.
options.C: As a key manifestation of the comprehensive strategic partnership between China and Algeria.
options.D: As a minor technical experiment with limited long-term impact on social development.

questions.explanation: 文章第四段引述习近平主席的话：the satellite project is an important manifestation of the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership。故C项符合题意。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements best describes the design and technical background of Alcomsat-1?

options.A: It was designed by international partners to ensure global compatibility.
options.B: It utilizes the DFH-4 satellite platform and was built by the China Academy of Space Technology.
options.C: It is an outdated model sold to Algeria at a discounted price.
options.D: It has a shorter lifespan compared to Western-made satellites.

questions.explanation: 文章第八段明确指出：Alcomsat-1 was designed and manufactured by the China Academy of Space Technology... based on the DFH-4 satellite platform。故B项正确。

questions.stem: According to the passage, what is the intended use of the Alcomsat-1 satellite?

options.A: Primarily for military reconnaissance and border surveillance.
options.B: Exclusively for television broadcasting and entertainment.
options.C: For a diverse range of public services including education and emergency communications.
options.D: For scientific research into the geostationary transfer orbit.

questions.explanation: 文章第九段提到：the satellite will be used for a wide variety of public services such as television broadcasting, emergency communications, remote education and satellite-based signal augmentation。故C项正确。

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the remarks made by Fu Zhiheng regarding the satellite's quality?

options.A: The satellite is technologically inferior to Western models but cost-effective.
options.B: The satellite is highly competitive and comparable to those produced in the West.
options.C: The satellite represents a collaborative effort incorporating Western technology.
options.D: The company struggles to match the standards set by Western space contractors.

questions.explanation: 文章最后一段指出：Fu Zhiheng... said Alcomsat-1 is as good as Western communications satellites in terms of technology and capacity。这说明其技术水平与西方卫星旗鼓相当。故B项正确。

papers.title: Satellite lofted for first Arab country
papers.body_text: Algeria's first communications satellite is launched on Monday. JU ZHENHUA/XINHUA A Chinese-made communications satellite became the first "export" of such a spacecraft to an Arab country when it was launched by China on Monday for Algeria. A Long March 3B carrier rocket blasted off at the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the southwestern province of Sichuan at 12:40 am. It soared for about 26 minutes before placing the Alcomsat-1, the Algeria's first communications satellite, into geostationary transfer orbit, according to a news release from China Great Wall Industry Corp, the project contractor. President Xi Jinping and his Algerian counterpart Abdelaziz Bouteflika exchanged congratulatory messages on Monday morning on the successful mission. In his message, Xi said that the satellite project is an important manifestation of the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership. It has become a good example of space cooperation between China and Arab countries and will play a significant role in promoting Algeria's economic and social development, he said. Xi said that Beijing is willing to work with Algeria to strengthen exchanges and cooperation in various fields, deepening the bilateral partnership so as to benefit the two countries and peoples. Bouteflika said that Alcomsat-1 is a remarkable achievement in space cooperation between the two countries and reflects the deep traditional friendship between the two sides. Algeria is willing to work with China to strengthen cooperation, he said. The satellite contract was signed in December 2013 between the Algerian Space Agency and China Great Wall Industry Corp, the country's largest space contractor in the international market. Alcomsat-1 was designed and manufactured by the China Academy of Space Technology, part of China Aerospace Science and Technology Corp, based on the DFH-4 satellite platform. With a liftoff weight of 5.2 metric tons and a designed life of 15 years, the satellite will be used for a wide variety of public services such as television broadcasting, emergency communications, remote education and satellite-based signal augmentation, according to Great Wall Industry. Fu Zhiheng, vice-president of the company, said Alcomsat-1 is as good as Western communications satellites in terms of technology and capacity. He said there are other contracts between his firm and Arab countries, without elaborating. zhaolei@chinadaily.com.cn

questions.stem: According to the passage, what is the primary significance of the Alcomsat-1 launch?

options.A: It marks the first time a Chinese satellite has been launched into orbit.
options.B: It represents the inaugural export of a Chinese communications satellite to an Arab nation.
options.C: It demonstrates the superiority of the DFH-4 satellite platform over Western technology.
options.D: It serves as the final step in the China-Algeria comprehensive strategic partnership.

questions.explanation: 文章第一段明确指出，该卫星是中国首次向阿拉伯国家出口的此类航天器，故选项B正确。

questions.stem: Which of the following can be inferred from the message exchanged between President Xi and President Bouteflika?

options.A: The mission was a result of a contract signed in early 2017.
options.B: The two countries intend to limit their cooperation to the aerospace sector.
options.C: Both leaders view the project as a milestone in strengthening bilateral relations.
options.D: The project was primarily initiated to compete with Western space technology.

questions.explanation: 文中两位领导人均表示该项目是中阿全面战略伙伴关系的体现，象征着友谊与进一步合作，这直接支持了选项C。

questions.stem: What is the technical background of the Alcomsat-1 satellite?

options.A: It was developed by a private European firm in partnership with China.
options.B: It is based on the DFH-4 platform and weighs over 5 metric tons.
options.C: It has an estimated lifespan of 10 years for public service operations.
options.D: It was launched from the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center.

questions.explanation: 文章提到该卫星由中国空间技术研究院设计制造，基于DFH-4平台，起飞重量为5.2公吨，选项B符合文意。

questions.stem: What is the intended use of the Alcomsat-1 satellite according to the text?

options.A: Exclusively for military surveillance and national security monitoring.
options.B: To facilitate diplomatic exchanges between China and Western nations.
options.C: To support various public services including education and emergency communication.
options.D: To serve as a test bed for future carrier rocket experimental flights.

questions.explanation: 文章最后一段详细列举了该卫星的用途，包括电视广播、应急通信、远程教育等公共服务，选项C与原文一致。

questions.stem: What does the phrase 'without elaborating' in the final paragraph suggest about the company's future?

options.A: The company is reluctant to disclose information due to legal complications.
options.B: The company has no further plans to cooperate with Arab countries.
options.C: The company prefers to maintain a low profile regarding its commercial strategies.
options.D: The company has other ongoing contracts but is keeping the specific details private.

questions.explanation: 作者提到副总裁Fu Zhiheng称公司与阿拉伯国家还有其他合同，但没有详细说明，这说明公司拥有其他合作项目，但拒绝透露具体细节。

papers.title: Would-be suicide bomber sets off explosion in NYC
papers.body_text: Police officers stand guard outside the New York Port Authority Bus Terminal in New York City, US December 11, 2017 after reports of an explosion. [Photo/Agencies] A would-be suicide bomber set off part of a homemade explosive device in a subway passage between Times Square and the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan during Monday's morning rush hour, seriously wounding himself and injuring three others, police said. The injuries to two commuters and a Port Authority police officer were all non-life threatening, according to the New York Fire Department. At a news conference, Mayor Bill de Blasio called the blast an attempted terrorist attack, and he said no other devices had been found. The suspect was identified by police as Akayed Ullah, 27, a Bangladeshi native, who lived in Brooklyn. He was in serious condition at Bellevue Hospital Center. Ullah acted alone, police said, and the device was reported to have gone off prematurely in the passage just 200 feet from the bus terminal at West 42nd Street and Eighth Avenue — one of the busiest transit hubs in the country — at 7:20 am, authorities said. Two commuters and a Port Authority police officer suffered minor injuries, officials said. The device was affixed to Ullah's body with Velcro and zip ties, police officials said. The explosion caused commuter chaos at one of the city's key traffic hubs. "Our lives revolve around the subway," the mayor said. "The choice of New York is always for a reason, because we are beacons of the world. And we show that a society of many background and many faiths can work." "The terrorists want to undermine that," he added. "They yearn to attack New York City." "I'm glad I wasn't there. We were supposed to take a bus out at the time (when the accident happened), so I'm glad we waited," said Monica Gannon, who planned to take the bus back to New Jersey at this morning when the explosion happened. "We are stuck here. Now we are waiting for all this to be over and go back home." "I was evacuated from the terminal after it (the explosion) happened. It was terrible, and I was so scared when I saw some people running,"said Alicia Carmona. She said she was evacuated from the terminal after the explosion and to wait outside the bus terminal for about two hours. Carmona said she planned to pick up her niece at LaGuardia Airport at around 9 am, but now she couldn't make it. "I was thinking to take the subway instead, but the nearby subway stations are closed too."

questions.stem: According to the passage, what can be inferred about the perpetrator's intention and the outcome of the explosion?

options.A: The terrorist successfully targeted the most crowded area of the bus terminal.
options.B: The explosion resulted in multiple fatalities, causing widespread panic.
options.C: The device detonated earlier than intended, resulting in limited casualties.
options.D: The suspect received immediate support from an accomplice in the subway passage.

questions.explanation: 文章提到“the device was reported to have gone off prematurely”，即装置提前爆炸，这解释了为何伤势并不致命，并未造成大规模伤亡。

questions.stem: Mayor Bill de Blasio's statement suggests that New York City is often targeted by terrorists because:

options.A: It has weak security measures at its transit hubs.
options.B: It serves as a symbolic representative of a diverse and functioning society.
options.C: It is the only city in the United States that allows for open expression of faith.
options.D: It frequently hosts high-profile international political conferences.

questions.explanation: 市长提到“we are beacons of the world. And we show that a society of many background and many faiths can work”，这说明纽约市因其多元融合的象征意义而成为目标。

questions.stem: How did the explosion affect the daily lives of commuters like Alicia Carmona?

options.A: She was physically injured and required immediate hospitalization.
options.B: She lost her belongings while running away from the bus terminal.
options.C: She was forced to abandon her plans due to evacuations and transit closures.
options.D: She successfully managed to reach the airport by using an alternative route.

questions.explanation: 文中Alicia Carmona提到她被疏散，且原本接侄女的计划被迫取消，附近地铁站也关闭了，说明出行计划受到了严重干扰。

questions.stem: What is the primary function of the phrase 'would-be' when describing the suicide bomber in the first paragraph?

options.A: To imply that the suspect was coerced by others into committing the act.
options.B: To highlight that the mission failed to achieve its ultimate destructive goal.
options.C: To suggest that the suspect regretted his decision at the last moment.
options.D: To emphasize that the suspect was not affiliated with any known terrorist organization.

questions.explanation: 'Would-be'在此处表示“未遂的”或“自封的”，结合上下文该袭击未造成致命后果且属于未遂的恐怖主义行为，旨在强调其目标未达成。

questions.stem: Which of the following best describes the tone of the eyewitness accounts in the article?

options.A: Indifferent and detached.
options.B: Anxious and disrupted.
options.C: Aggressive and defensive.
options.D: Optimistic and hopeful.

questions.explanation: 目击者描述中使用了“scared”、“stuck”、“terrible”等词汇，表现出袭击事件对其正常生活造成的惊慌与干扰，故选B。
